reasoning: The Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim. It asserts that there exists a natural number p and a Sylow p-subgroup P of G such that p is prime, p divides card G (= 351), and P is normal — which is exactly "G has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The hypotheses `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G` correctly restrict p to primes dividing |G| (necessary because `Sylow p G` is defined in Mathlib for arbitrary p, with the trivial subgroup serving as the Sylow p-subgroup when p does not divide |G|). The `Sylow` type from Mathlib denotes a maximal p-subgroup, and `P.Normal` is the standard normality condition on the underlying subgroup. No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what the informal statement entails, so the formalization is faithful.
